data.title: Molecular Partners and Orano Med Announce First Patients Dosed in Phase 1/2a Trial of DLL3 Radio-DARPin MP0712
data.description: In a recent announcement on July 2, 2026, Molecular Partners AG (SIX: MOLN; NASDAQ: MOLN), based in Zurich-Schlieren, Switzerland, and Orano Med from Concord, Mass., along with Villejuif, France, reported that the initial patients have been administered doses in the current US multicenter Phase 1/2a study for the drug candidate MP0712. Molecular Partners, a clinical-stage biotech company specializing in DARPin therapeutics, and Orano Med, a clinical-stage radiopharmaceutical company focusing on lead-212 (212 Pb) targeted alpha therapies (TAT), joined forces for this development. This collaboration marks a significant milestone in their continued efforts towards the advancement of innovative protein drugs and radiation-based therapeutic techniques.

data.title: Novartis receives European Commission approval for Itvisma® for spinal muscular atrophy (SMA)
data.description: On June 30, 2026, Novartis revealed that the European Commission has given the green light to Itvisma® (onasemnogene abeparvovec) for treating individuals aged 2 and above, including children, teenagers, and adults, who are affected by 5q spinal muscular atrophy (SMA) and possess a dual mutation in the survival motor neuron 1 (SMN1) gene.

data.title: [Ad hoc announcement pursuant to Art. 53 LR] Roche’s divarasib shows superiority in head-to-head phase III trial against approved KRAS G12C inhibitors in non-small cell lung cancer
data.description: Roche revealed today positive outcomes from the phase III Krascendo 1 trial assessing divarasib, a novel KRAS G12C inhibitor, versus established first-generation KRAS G12C inhibitors sotorasib or adagrasib in individuals with prior treatment for KRAS G12C non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC). The research successfully reached its primary and key secondary goal, demonstrating divarasib's significant enhancements in both progression-free survival (PFS) and overall survival (OS). The safety profile of divarasib remained in line with previous data, showing no new discoveries and the most frequent treatment-related side effects were controllable and reversible.

data.title: Dow Jones Futures: Stock Market Rallies On U.S.-Iran News; Alphabet, Rocket Lab, SpaceX, Tesla Are Big Winners
data.description: Stock futures for the Dow Jones Industrial Average rose on Monday following positive developments in U.S.-Iran relations. The stock market saw a significant surge, with notable gains in companies like Alphabet, Rocket Lab, SpaceX, and Tesla. These companies experienced a considerable uptick in their stock prices as a result of the positive news. The overall market response to the U.S.-Iran news was favorable, leading to a bullish trend in trading. This surge in stock prices indicates investor confidence in the market.

data.title: Stock Market Today: Dow Rises To Record; Cathie Wood Buys This Software Stock Amid 46% Dive
data.description: Today, there was a notable increase in the Dow Jones on the stock market. President Trump's attempt to dismiss Lisa Cook was thwarted by the Supreme Court. Meanwhile, Cathie Wood made a purchase of a plummeting stock, and SpaceX experienced significant growth. The Dow reached a record high, and Wood invested in a software stock that had fallen by 46%. This information was reported in the Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Anthropic reactivates AI model Fable 5 worldwide post U.S. export ban lifted, with upgraded security and AI jailbreak guidelines.
data.description: Anthropic has expanded the global distribution of Fable 5 following the removal of export restrictions in the U.S. The company has included additional protective measures and established new AI jailbreak guidelines across the industry.
